description: Original baseline re-run at 1bps cost. Still awful — strategy is broken, not costs.
Cross-sectional momentum L3/S3, calibrated stops, VIX scaling
SPY trend tilt: long-only in uptrend, short-only in downtrend, balanced in sideways. 2xATR stops.
Buy-the-dip in uptrend, sell-the-rally in downtrend. 1.5x stop, 2.5x target. 8/19 positive.
No fallback, 5d regime, tighter thresholds, higher trend minimum. 10/19 positive.
MLP trained on features->intraday sign. First positive Sharpe! Very selective, low trade count.
NN with lower confidence threshold (0.53). Trading more but losing edge. NN only works when selective.
Hybrid NN+rules: too restrictive requiring both to agree. Trades only 0.6/day.
Larger NN + more features + weighted loss. Overfitting killed it.
Ensemble of 5 logistic regressions. Less overfitting but less signal than NN.
NN on recent 500 days + 0.56/0.44 thresholds. More trades but diluted signal.
NN cross-sectional: long top-2 short bot-2 by prob. Short side bleeds in bull markets.
Regression NN predicting return magnitude + SPY regime filter. Much worse than v11 classification. Regime too strict (0 trades in slice 15).
Classification NN + soft regime bias on thresholds. Regime shift hurts — worse than v11 baseline.
Ensemble of 3 NNs (seeds 42/123/7) averaged. Close to v11 but slightly worse — averaging dilutes best model.
Tight TP (0.25x ATR) + wide SL (1x ATR). Win rate up to 38% but asymmetric R:R destroys returns. Losses 4x wins.
Train on actual trade outcomes (0.5x TP/1.5x SL). Win rate up to 44% but asymmetric R:R still kills returns.
Rich candlestick features (17 total) + symmetric 1x ATR TP/SL. Win rate up to 28% but not enough edge.
Long-only NN with rich features. Most consistent ever — all recent slices positive. But win rate still 32%.
Very wide 3x ATR TP/SL, pure close-exit. Win rate ~50% in bull slices but destroyed in bears without stop protection.
NEW BEST Sharpe! Long-only + cash in bear (spy_mom<-2%). But too few trades (0.4/day), 4 zero-trade slices.
Loosened bear filter (-4% vs -2%) + lower threshold (0.55). Much worse — bad trades flood back in.
Always-invested long portfolio (top 8 stocks daily). WIN RATE >50% for first time! But negative returns — losses too large in bears.
Portfolio with v11 stops (1.5x/2x ATR). Win rate 52% but tighter stops add more stop-outs, returns still negative.
BREAKTHROUGH: SPY core (0.3) + NN satellite picks. First strategy with positive Sharpe + win rate >50% + positive returns.
Flat in severe bear, reduced core in mild bear. Worse than v30 — removed profitable bounce days.
Same as v30 but wider SPY core stops (5x ATR). Slightly worse — v30 SPY stops provide useful protection.
v26 selectivity + 0.15 SPY core. Great Sharpe but win rate 42.7% not >50%.
v30 with tighter satellite threshold (0.60). Sharpe dropped — too few satellites to drive alpha.
v30 with looser satellite threshold (0.56). More noise, not more alpha. v30 (0.58) is the sweet spot.
Zero fee L5/S5 cross-sectional. 10 trades/day, 52% win rate. Strong base to build on.
L10/S10 with zero fees. Sharpe=0.73! More diversification = better risk-adjusted returns.
Long/short ALL 28 stocks. Max diversification but diluted signal. v37 L10/S10 has better edge.
DualHead (direction+magnitude) with confidence-weighted L10/S10. Worse than v37 — conf weighting dilutes diversification.
Pure gap-fade mean reversion (no NN). Inconsistent — works in some slices, destroyed in others. 30% win rate.
Deeper NN (256->128->64) + gap/range features. Overfits — more capacity hurts. v37 simpler arch is better.
v37 with 10x ATR SL/TP (hold to close). Worse Sharpe — v37's asymmetric stops add value.
v37 + exponential recency weighting (halflife=252d). Near-identical to v37. Recency is neutral.
NEW BEST! Huber regression predicting returns > BCE classification for ranking. +0.12 Sharpe, +45% return vs v37.
